zh.lifeSummary: 當Henry or Henri de la Peyre於1154年出生在英國英格蘭倫敦時，他的父親Richard de la Peyre34歲，母親Lady Richard de la Peyre29歲。他和Lady Henry de la Peyre至少有1個兒子。他去世於1189年享年35歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Henry or Henri de la Peyre于1154年出生在英国英格兰倫敦时，他的父亲Richard de la Peyre34岁，母亲Lady Richard de la Peyre29岁。他和Lady Henry de la Peyre至少有1个儿子。他去世于1189年享年35岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Peyre
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Peter  .
Americanized form of German   Petri  .
Swedish (mainly Petré): shortened form of Petraeus, a Latinized patronymic from the personal name Per, Pär (see Peter ).

variants.name: Petrea
meanings: Romanian: from a derivative of the personal name Petre or Petru , equivalents of Peter , formed with the suffix -ea.

variants.name: Petrescu
meanings: Romanian: patronymic, with the suffix -escu, from the personal name Petre or Petru , equivalents of Peter .

variants.name: Peter
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Ernst, Fritz, Heinz, Helmut, Horst, Kaspar, Klaus, Siegfried, Wolfgang.

English, Scottish, German, Dutch, French (Alsace and Lorraine), Czech (Moravian), Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian; Hungarian (Péter): from the personal name Peter (Greek Petros, from petra ‘rock’). The personal name was popular throughout Christian Europe in the Middle Ages, having been bestowed by Christ as a byname on the apostle Simon bar Jonah, the brother of Andrew. The name was chosen by Christ for its symbolic significance (John 1:42, Matt. 16:18); Saint Peter is regarded as the founding head of the Christian Church in view of Christ's saying, ‘Thou art Peter and upon this rock I will build my Church’. In Christian Germany in the early Middle Ages this was the most frequent personal name of non-ancient Germanic origin until the 14th century. In North America, this surname has also absorbed cognates from other languages, for example Czech Petr , Polish Piotr and Pietr, Albanian Pjetri (from the personal name Pjetër, definite form Pjetri), and also their derivatives (see examples at Peterson ). It has also been adopted as a surname by Ashkenazic Jews.

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

nameMeanings.name: or
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

nameMeanings.name: Henri
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Germanic. See Henry .

variants.name: Henry
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

en.lifeSummary: When Henry or Henri de la Peyre was born in 1154, in London, England, United Kingdom, his father, Richard de la Peyre, was 34 and his mother, Lady Richard de la Peyre, was 29. He had at least 1 son with Lady Henry de la Peyre. He died in 1189, at the age of 35.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Henry or Henri de la Peyre est né en 1154, à Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Richard de la Peyre, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Lady Richard de la Peyre, avait 29 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Lady Henry de la Peyre. Il est décédé en 1189, à l’âge de 35 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Henry or Henri de la Peyre 1154 in London, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard de la Peyre 34 und seine Mutter Lady Richard de la Peyre 29. Er hatte mit Lady Henry de la Peyre mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1189 im Alter von 35 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Henry or Henri de la Peyre è nato il nel 1154 nel London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Richard de la Peyre, aveva 34 anni e il suo madre, Lady Richard de la Peyre, aveva 29 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Lady Henry de la Peyre. Morì nel 1189, all'età di 35 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Henry or Henri de la Peyreが1154年にイギリスイギリスロンドンで生まれた時，父Richard de la Peyreは34歳，母Lady Richard de la Peyreは29歳であった。彼はLady Henry de la Peyreとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1189年に35歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Henry or Henri de la Peyre(이)가 1154년 영국 잉글랜드 런던에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard de la Peyre(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Richard de la Peyre(은)는 29세였다. 그는 Lady Henry de la Peyre(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1189년 35세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Henry or Henri de la Peyre nasceu em 1154, em London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Richard de la Peyre, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Lady Richard de la Peyre, tinha 29 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Lady Henry de la Peyre. Ele faleceu em 1189, com 35 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Henry or Henri de la Peyre родился в 1154 г. в местности Лондон, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Richard de la Peyre было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Richard de la Peyre, 29. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Lady Henry de la Peyre. Он умер в 1189 г. в возрасте 35 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Henry or Henri de la Peyre nació en 1154, en London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Richard de la Peyre, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Lady Richard de la Peyre, tenía 29 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Lady Henry de la Peyre. Murió en 1189, a la edad de 35 años.
